figures.caption: Table 3: Demographics for the analysis sample (n = 114). Continuous variables are summarized as mean (standard deviation), and categorical variables are presented as counts (percentages).

Figure 2: Partial-correlation-based functional connectivity (with p = 400) for two participants (NC and dementia) based on estimated precision matrices using glasso, adaptive glasso, MCP, and SCAD. Negative partial correlations were excluded, and a square-root transformation was applied to improve visualization contrast. The sparsity is indicated in the subtitle of each panel.
Table 2: Performance of the surveyed methods with a fixed sample size of n = 180 based on 100 simulation replicates.

Figure 1: Boxplots of the estimation performance metrics (Frobenius norm, Kullback–Leibler divergence, and F1 score) for all methods across different network dimensions (with fixed n = 180), based on 100 simulation replicates.
